title: Hilairanthus germinans
taxonomy: Hilairanthus germinans is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Achariaceae. It is a member of the genus Hilairanthus, which is a part of the order Malpighiales. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, including the Caribbean and parts of Mexico. The classification of Hilairanthus germinans is further supported by its botanical name, which is derived from the Greek words for "mallow" and "tree". The plant's classification reflects its unique characteristics and its relationship to other flowering plants.
